name: @forwardimpact/jidoka
description: Build quality into agent instructions — the jidoka CLI and CI action stop the line the moment an instruction layer drifts, a jobs block goes stale, or a repository invariant breaks.
author: D. Olsson <hi@senzilla.io>

jobs.trigger: The team's layered instructions exist, but nothing enforces them — layers drift and restate each other, and stale jobs blocks ship unnoticed until an agent misbehaves.
jobs.bigHire: keep humans and agents on one layered instruction architecture, with checks that stop the line the moment a layer drifts.
jobs.littleHire: check one layer's caps, validate the jobs blocks, or run the repository's invariant rules with a single command before commit.
jobs.competesWith: unenforced conventions; review-time nitpicking; hand-rolled lint scripts; letting drift accumulate until a rewrite

forces.push: Instruction sprawl keeps breaking agent behavior with no layer to blame.
forces.pull: One command family and CI action that stop the line at the first drifted layer.
forces.habit: Trusting contributors to keep instructions tidy by hand.

jobs.firedWhen: the checks block more work than the drift they catch, or the team stops layering its instructions.
